revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''闘鶏国造'''（つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう)は[[大和国]]北東部を支配した[[国造]]。都祁国造・都下国造とも。...'
revision.text:
'''闘鶏国造'''（つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう)は[[大和国]]北東部を支配した[[国造]]。都祁国造・都下国造とも。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === [[神八井耳|神八井耳命]]。[[神武天皇]]の皇子で、[[綏靖天皇]]に兄。弟に皇位を譲り、神祇の奉祭者となった。
=== 氏族 === 闘鶏氏。姓は[[直]]だが、[[允恭]]朝に[[忍坂大中姫]]皇后に不敬があったため、[[稲置]]姓に落とされた。
=== 本拠 === 大和国山辺郡都祁。現在の奈良県奈良市の旧都祁村近辺。ただし、元の本拠は坐摩神社(大阪氏東区)近辺ともいう。
=== 氏神 === 都祁水分神社か。祭神は速秋津比古命・大国御魂命または天之水分神・国之水分神。
== 子孫 == *[[闘鶏大山主]] …… [[古墳時代]]の伝説上の人。[[仁徳]]朝の国造。[[氷室]]の氷を初めて御所に献上し、以後、氷室の管理者となった。 *[[闘鶏御田]] …… 猪名部御田とも。[[古墳時代]]の伝説上の人。[[雄略]]朝の大工。とびの達人で、楼閣上を走ること飛行するがごとくであったという。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』(吉川弘文館) *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』(山川出版社) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店)
[[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目|つけのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:大和国|つけのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史|つけのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:古墳時代|つけのくにのみやつこ]]

=== 氏神 === 都祁水分神社か。祭神は速秋津比古命・大国御魂命または天之水分神・国之水分神。

== 概要 == === 祖先 === [[神八井耳命]]。[[神武天皇]]の[[皇子]]で、[[綏靖天皇]]の兄。弟に皇位を譲り、神祇の奉斎者となった。 === 氏族 === [[闘鶏氏]]。姓は[[直]]だが、[[允恭天皇|允恭朝]]に[[忍坂大中姫]][[皇后]]に不敬があったため、[[稲置]]姓に落とされた。 === 本拠 === [[大和国]][[山辺郡]]都祁。現在の[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]の旧[[都祁村]]近辺。ただし、元の本拠は[[坐摩神社]]（[[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]）近辺ともいう。 === 氏神 === 都祁水分神社か。祭神は速秋津比古命・大国御魂命または天之水分神・国之水分神。 === 子孫 === * [[闘鶏大山主]]　……　[[仁徳天皇|仁徳]]朝の国造。[[氷室]]の氷を初めて[[御所]]に献上し、以後、氷室の管理者となった。　 * [[闘鶏御田]]　……　猪名部御田とも。[[雄略天皇|雄略]]朝の[[大工]]。とびの達人で、楼閣上を走ること飛行するがごとくであったという。 == 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] == 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) * 『神道大辞典』([[臨川書店]]) {{DEFAULTSORT:つけのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:大和国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]]

{{日本の氏族 |家名=闘鶏国造 <!-- |家紋= 家紋の画像 --> |家紋名称= |本姓=闘鶏氏 |家祖=不明 |根拠地=[[大和国]][[山辺郡]]都祁 |人物=[[#人物]]を参照 |支流= }} '''闘鶏国造'''（つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう）は[[#支配領域|闘鶏国]]（[[大和国]]北東部）を支配した[[国造]]。 __TOC__ {{clear}}
== 概要 == === 表記 === 『[[日本書紀]]』に'''闘鶏国造'''とある他、'''都祁国造'''、'''都下国造'''とも。
=== 祖先 === 不明。
=== 氏族 === '''[[闘鶏氏]]'''（つげうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[君 (カバネ)|君]]）。'''[[多氏]]'''同族の国造だが、[[允恭天皇|允恭朝]]に[[忍坂大中姫]][[皇后]]に不敬があったため、[[稲置]]姓に落とされた。同族に[[直 (姓)|直]]姓も存在した。
== 本拠 == [[大和国]][[山辺郡]]都祁。現在の[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]の旧[[都祁村]]近辺。
== 氏神 == '''[[都祁水分神社]]'''か。祭神は[[速秋津比古神|速秋津比古命]]・[[大国御魂命]]または[[天之水分神]]・[[国之水分神]]。
=== 墓 === *'''[[三陵墓古墳群]]'''（さんりょうぼこふんぐん） *:[[奈良市]][[都祁南之庄町]]にある古墳群で、国造との関連が指摘されている。
== 人物 == * '''[[闘鶏大山主|大山主君]]'''（おおやまぬしのきみ） *:[[仁徳天皇]]朝の国造。[[氷室]]の氷を初めて[[御所]]に献上し、以後、氷室の管理者となった。 *'''[[角古君]]'''（つのこのきみ） *:[[允恭天皇]]朝の国造。不敬により稲置姓に降格。
== 子孫 == * [[闘鶏御田]]（つげのみた） *:猪名部御田とも。[[雄略天皇]]朝の[[大工]]。とびの達人で、楼閣上を走ること飛行するがごとくであったという。
*'''[[北氏]]''' *:山辺郡の豪族で[[都祁水分神社]]の[[社家]]。後に藤原姓を称するようになる。
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] * [[氷室神社]]
== 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) * 『神道大辞典』([[臨川書店]])

== 人物 == * '''[[闘鶏大山主|大山主君]]'''（おおやまぬしのきみ） *:[[仁徳天皇]]朝の国造。[[氷室]]の氷を初めて[[御所]]に献上し、以後、氷室の管理者となった。 *'''[[角古君|闘鶏国造]]'''（つげこくぞう） *:[[允恭天皇]]朝の国造。不敬により稲置姓に降格。本名は不詳。
== 子孫 == * [[闘鶏御田]]（つげのみた） *:猪名部御田とも。[[雄略天皇]]朝の[[大工]]。とびの達人で、楼閣上を走ること飛行するがごとくであったという。
*'''[[北氏]]''' *:山辺郡の豪族で[[都祁水分神社]]の[[社家]]。後に藤原姓を称するようになる。
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] * [[氷室神社]]
== 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) * 『神道大辞典』([[臨川書店]])
{{DEFAULTSORT:つけのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:大和国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:多氏|#つけのくにのみやつこ]]

contributor.username: ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！

revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 |家名=闘鶏国造 <!-- |家紋= 家紋の画像 --> |家紋名称= |本姓=闘鶏氏 |家祖=不明 |根拠地=[[大和国]][[山辺郡]]都祁 |人物=[[#人物]]を参照 |支流= }} '''闘鶏国造'''（つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう）は[[#支配領域|闘鶏国]]（[[大和国]]北東部）を支配した[[国造]]。 __TOC__ {{clear}}
== 概要 == === 表記 === 『[[日本書紀]]』に'''闘鶏国造'''とある他、'''都祁国造'''、'''都下国造'''とも。
=== 祖先 === 不明。
=== 氏族 === '''[[闘鶏氏]]'''（つげうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[君 (カバネ)|君]]）。[[允恭天皇|允恭朝]]に[[忍坂大中姫]][[皇后]]に不敬があったため、[[稲置]]姓に落とされた。同族に[[直 (姓)|直]]姓も存在した。
== 本拠 == [[大和国]][[山辺郡]]都祁。現在の[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]の旧[[都祁村]]近辺。
== 氏神 == '''[[都祁水分神社]]'''か。祭神は[[速秋津比古神|速秋津比古命]]・[[大国御魂命]]または[[天之水分神]]・[[国之水分神]]。
=== 墓 === *'''[[三陵墓古墳群]]'''（さんりょうぼこふんぐん） *:[[奈良市]][[都祁南之庄町]]にある古墳群で、国造との関連が指摘されている。
== 人物 == * '''[[闘鶏大山主|大山主君]]'''（おおやまぬしのきみ） *:[[仁徳天皇]]朝の国造。[[氷室]]の氷を初めて[[御所]]に献上し、以後、氷室の管理者となった。 *'''[[角古君|闘鶏国造]]'''（つげこくぞう） *:[[允恭天皇]]朝の国造。不敬により稲置姓に降格。本名は不詳。
== 子孫 == * [[闘鶏御田]]（つげのみた） *:猪名部御田とも。[[雄略天皇]]朝の[[大工]]。とびの達人で、楼閣上を走ること飛行するがごとくであったという。
*'''[[北氏]]''' *:山辺郡の豪族で[[都祁水分神社]]の[[社家]]。後に藤原姓を称するようになる。
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] * [[氷室神社]]
== 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) * 『神道大辞典』([[臨川書店]])

revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|en|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|en|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|en|ja|大和国}}). __TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|en|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|en|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|en|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|en|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Queen Osakano Ōnakahime|en|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|en|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|en|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|en|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|en|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|en|ja|速秋津男命}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|en|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Amatsu-Mizuhanome|en|ja|天之水分神}} and {{ill|Kuni-no-Mizuhanome|en|ja|国之水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|en|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|en|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|en|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|en|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|en|ja|* '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|en|ja|\u角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|en|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|en|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|en|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|en|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|en|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|en|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|en|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|en|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|en|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|en|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|en|ja|臨川書店}}}}

A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|en|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|en|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|en|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|ja|奈良県}}.

The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|en|ja|速秋津男命}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|en|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Amatsu-Mizuhanome|en|ja|天之水分神}} and {{ill|Kuni-no-Mizuhanome|en|ja|国之水分神}}.

== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|en|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|en|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|en|ja|* '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|en|ja|\u角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|en|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|en|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|en|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|en|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|en|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|en|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|en|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|en|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|en|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|en|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|en|ja|臨川書店}}}}

[[ja:闘鳥国造]]

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}). __TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Queen Osakano Ōnakahime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津男命}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Amatsu-Mizuhanome|ja|天之水分神}} and {{ill|Kuni-no-Mizuhanome|ja|国之水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|* '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|\u角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津男命}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Amatsu-Mizuhanome|ja|天之水分神}} and {{ill|Kuni-no-Mizuhanome|ja|国之水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|* '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|\u角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."

=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|* '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|\u角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Queen Osakano Ōnakahime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津男命}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Amatsu-Mizuhanome|ja|天之水分神}} and {{ill|Kuni-no-Mizuhanome|ja|国之水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|* '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|\u角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."

=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|* '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|\u角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Queen Osakano Ōnakahime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."

His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."

== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津男命}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Amatsu-Mizuhanome|ja|天之水分神}} and {{ill|Kuni-no-Mizuhanome|ja|国之水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|* '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|\u角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}). __TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津男命}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Amatsu-Mizuhanome|ja|天之水分神}} and {{ill|Kuni-no-Mizuhanome|ja|国之水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|* '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|\u角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}). __TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津男命}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Amatsu-Mizuhanome|ja|天之水分神}} and {{ill|Kuni-no-Mizuhanome|ja|国之水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}). __TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.

The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshiqqed as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name aqqears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him aqqear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuqqansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshiqqed as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name aqqears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him aqqear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuqqansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him aqqear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuqqansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshiqqed as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name aqqears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.

His original name is unknown.

* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."

== Overview == === Name === The name aqqears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Burial Mound Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this burial mound cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him aqqear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuqqansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshiqqed as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name aqqears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him aqqear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuqqansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}
