{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.

== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara."== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店}}}}

== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|12=draft}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|12=draft}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|12=draft}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|12=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=draft}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社|12=draft}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店|12=draft}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest Image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|12=draft}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|12=draft}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|12=draft}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|12=draft}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|12=draft}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|12=draft}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|12=draft}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫|12=draft}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|12=draft}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|12=draft}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=draft}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|12=draft}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|12=draft}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|12=draft}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|12=draft}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室|12=draft}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|12=draft}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|12=draft}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|12=draft}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|12=draft}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|12=draft}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|12=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=draft}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社|12=draft}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店|12=draft}}}}

== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店|12=draft}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|12=draft}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|12=draft}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|12=draft}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|12=draft}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|12=draft}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|12=draft}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|12=draft}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫|12=draft}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|12=draft}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|12=draft}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=draft}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|12=draft}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|12=draft}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|12=draft}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|12=draft}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室|12=draft}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|12=draft}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|12=draft}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|12=draft}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|12=draft}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|12=draft}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|12=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=draft}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社|12=draft}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店|12=draft}}}}

{{draft categories| [[Category:History of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuge Clan]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:Totsukaze]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Spp]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:闘鳥国造]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = <!-- Image of crest --> |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|12=draft}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|12=draft}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|12=draft}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|12=draft}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|12=draft}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|12=draft}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|12=draft}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫|12=draft}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|12=draft}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|12=draft}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=draft}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|12=draft}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|12=draft}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|12=draft}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|12=draft}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室|12=draft}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|12=draft}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|12=draft}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|12=draft}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|12=draft}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|12=draft}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|12=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=draft}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社|12=draft}}
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|12=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店|12=draft}}}}

revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 |家名=闘鶏国造 <!-- |家紋= 家紋の画像 --> |家紋名称= |本姓=闘鶏氏 |家祖=不明 |根拠地=[[大和国]][[山辺郡]]都祁 |人物=[[#人物]]を参照 |支流= }} '''闘鶏国造'''（つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう）は[[#支配領域|闘鶏国]]（[[大和国]]北東部）を支配した[[国造]]。 __TOC__ {{clear}}
== 概要 == === 表記 === 『[[日本書紀]]』に'''闘鶏国造'''とある他、'''都祁国造'''、'''都下国造'''とも。
=== 祖先 === 不明。
=== 氏族 === '''[[闘鶏氏]]'''（つげうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[君 (カバネ)|君]]）。[[允恭天皇|允恭朝]]に[[忍坂大中姫]][[皇后]]に不敬があったため、[[稲置]]姓に落とされた。同族に[[直 (姓)|直]]姓も存在した。
== 本拠 == [[大和国]][[山辺郡]]都祁。現在の[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]の旧[[都祁村]]近辺。
== 氏神 == '''[[都祁水分神社]]'''か。祭神は[[速秋津比古神|速秋津比古命]]・[[大国御魂命]]または[[天之水分神]]・[[国之水分神]]。
=== 墓 === *'''[[三陵墓古墳群]]'''（さんりょうぼこふんぐん） *:[[奈良市]][[都祁南之庄町]]にある古墳群で、国造との関連が指摘されている。
== 人物 == * '''[[闘鶏大山主|大山主君]]'''（おおやまぬしのきみ） *:[[仁徳天皇]]朝の国造。[[氷室]]の氷を初めて[[御所]]に献上し、以後、氷室の管理者となった。 *'''[[角古君|闘鶏国造]]'''（つげこくぞう） *:[[允恭天皇]]朝の国造。不敬により稲置姓に降格。本名は不詳。
== 子孫 == * [[闘鶏御田]]（つげのみた） *:猪名部御田とも。[[雄略天皇]]朝の[[大工]]。とびの達人で、楼閣上を走ること飛行するがごとくであったという。
*'''[[北氏]]''' *:山辺郡の豪族で[[都祁水分神社]]の[[社家]]。後に藤原姓を称するようになる。
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] * [[氷室神社]]
== 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』([[吉川弘文館]]) * 『日本史広辞典』([[山川出版社]]) * 『神道大辞典』([[臨川書店]])
{{DEFAULTSORT:つけのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:大和国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:多氏|#つけのくにのみやつこ]]

== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}}

=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}}

During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ingyō|qq=}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|qq=}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe District|qq=}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Village|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro|qq=}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ingyō|qq=}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe District|qq=}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Shrine|qq=}}
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])

=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ingyō|qq=}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|qq=}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe District|qq=}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Village|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro|qq=}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ingyō|qq=}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe District|qq=}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Shrine|qq=}}
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe District|qq=}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ingyō|qq=}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|qq=}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe District|qq=}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Village|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro|qq=}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ingyō|qq=}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe District|qq=}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Shrine|qq=}}
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe District|qq=}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ingyō|qq=}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|qq=}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe District|qq=}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Village|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro|qq=}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ingyō|qq=}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe District|qq=}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Shrine|qq=}}
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])
