{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}{{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe District|qq=}}, Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ingyō|qq=}}, the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=}}" due to disrespect shown to {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|qq=}}. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe District|qq=}}, in the area of modern-day {{ill|Tsuge Village|ja|都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Village|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}, credited as the first to present ice from the {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro|qq=}} (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ingyō|qq=}}, demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=}}''' *: A powerful family in {{ill|Yamabe District|ja|山辺郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe District|qq=}} associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Shrine|qq=}}
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])

== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])

revision.text:
{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]][[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]][[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==

=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])

The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])

=== Ancestors === Unknown.

During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])

* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. == Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])

* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinsen Shoten|qq=}}}}

{{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]][[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. == Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]

* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.

== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].

The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinsen Shoten|qq=}}}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11656591]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]][[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).

__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.

His original name is unknown.

They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
