== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]

During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ|1=Declan Rice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q161426}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=|1=Tsunoko Kimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87864913}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=|1=Kita Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402293}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])

=== Ancestors === Unknown.

During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ|1=Oryza sativa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q161426}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. === Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11656591]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]][[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ|1=Oryza sativa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q161426}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=|1=Tsunoko Kimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87864913}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=|1=Kita Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402293}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])

They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11656591]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]][[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ|1=Oryza sativa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q161426}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=|1=Tsunoko Kimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87864913}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=|1=Kita Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402293}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11656591]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]][[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ|1=Oryza sativa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q161426}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=|1=Tsunoko Kimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87864913}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=|1=Kita Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402293}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])
== References == * {{Cite book|title=國史大辞典|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Tarō Sakamoto, Kunio Hirano|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Name Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川学文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese History Dictionary|publisher={{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shuppansha|qq=}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Shintō Encyclopedia|publisher={{ill|Rinsen Shoten|ja|臨川書店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinsen Shoten|qq=|1=Rinsen Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612454}}}}

* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11656591]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]][[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ|1=Oryza sativa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q161426}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|qq=|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|qq=|1=Tsunoko Kimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87864913}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|qq=|1=Kita Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402293}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])

=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11656591]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]][[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).

== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ|1=Oryza sativa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q161426}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Ame no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.

{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].

* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.

* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.

== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11656591]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]][[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ|1=Oryza sativa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q161426}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Ame no Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|1=Tsunoko Kimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87864913}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|1=Kita Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402293}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}

Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
== See Also ==
* [[List of Kunizuko]] * [[Himuro Shrine]]
== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * Sakamoto Tarō & Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]) * ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]])

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11656591]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clans |Name = Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest image = |Crest Name = |Origin Surname = Tsuge Clan |Ancestor = Unknown |Base Territory = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]][[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], Tsuge |Notable Figures = See [[#Figures|Figures]] |Branches = }} '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (つげのくにのみやつこ・つげこくぞう) governed the [[#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).
* {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} was a notable figure and is worshipped as an ice god
__TOC__ {{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name appears as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], as well as '''Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and '''Tsuge Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.
=== Ancestors === Unknown.
=== Clan === The clan is the '''{{ill|Tsuge Clan|ja|闘鳥氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuge-shi) with the {{ill|Kabane|ja|委姻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}." During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], the clan was demoted to the kabane "{{ill|Inagi|ja|稲穂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イネ|1=Oryza sativa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q161426}}" due to disrespect shown to [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]]. A related branch held the kabane "{{ill|Atoneri|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atoneri|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}."
== Base Territory == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]], in the area of modern-day [[:en:Tsuge, Nara|Tsuge Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been '''{{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}'''. The shrine’s deities are identified as {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速秋津男命|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja|大国寡魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni-no-Mitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, or {{ill|Mikumari|ja|天之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Ame no Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} and {{ill|Mikumari|ja|国之水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}}.
=== Burial Site === * '''{{ill|Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja|三陵墓古坛群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryōbo Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Located in {{ill|Tsuge Minaminosho|ja|都祁南の莊町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Minaminosho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], this Kofun cluster is believed to be associated with the clan.
== Figures == * '''{{ill|Ōyamanu Kimi|ja|大山主君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamanu Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: A governor during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仲徹天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, credited as the first to present ice from the [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial court, establishing the tradition of managing the icehouses. * '''{{ill|Tsunoko Kimi|ja|角古君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunoko Kimi|1=Tsunoko Kimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87864913}} (Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko)''' *: A governor during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], demoted to the kabane "Inagi" due to disrespect. His original name is unknown.
== Descendants == * '''{{ill|Tsuge no Mita|ja|闘鳥御田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Mita|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' *: Also known as {{ill|Inabe no Mita|ja|伊名部御田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inabe no Mita|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he was a master craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. His skill in high-altitude construction made him appear to "fly like a bird."
* '''{{ill|Kita Clan|ja|北氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Clan|1=Kita Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402293}}''' *: A powerful family in [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamabe District]] associated with the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} as its priestly line. They later adopted the surname "Fujiwara." == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]]
== Overview ==
{{Japanese clans|name=Tsuge no Kunizuko|crest_name=|ancestral_name=Tsuge-shi|founder=Unknown|location=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]] (Tsuge)|notable_people=See [[#Notable Figures]]|branches=}} '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{transl|ja|つげのくにのみやつこ}}・{{transl|ja|つげこくぞう}}) was a [[Kunizuko]] (provincial governor) that governed [[Tōkei no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Territory|Tsuge Province]] (northeastern [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]).{{clear}}
== Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as '''Tsuge no Kunizuko''' in the [[Nihon Shoki]], but it is also referred to as '''Tsuge no Kuni''' or '''Toshimo Kuni'''.
=== Ancestry === The ancestor of the clan is unknown.
=== Clan === The '''[[Tsuge Clan]]''' ({{lang-ja|闘鶏氏}}, {{transl|ja|つげうじ}}) bore the title of [[Kimi (Kabane)|Kimi]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]], they were demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] title due to misconduct against Empress [[Osakanaka no Ōnakahime]]. Members of the clan also held the [[Naohi (Kabane)|Naohi]] title.
== Headquarters == The clan was based in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[Yamabe District]], in the region of Tsuge, corresponding to the former [[Tsuge Village]] in present-day [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Clan Deities == The clan deity may have been associated with '''[[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]'''. The enshrined deities include [[Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]], [[Ame no Mikumari no Kami]], and [[Kuni no Mikumari no Kami]].
=== Tombs ===
* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.

* {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanryobo Kofun Group|qq=}} *: Located in [[Tsuge Minaminosho Town]], [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]]. This group of tumuli is believed to have connections with the Tsuge no Kunizuko.
== Notable Figures ==
* '''[[Ōyamanushi Kimi]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. He was the first to offer ice from the [[Himuro]] (icehouse) to the imperial palace, thus becoming its custodian.
* '''[[Kakuko Kimi|Tsuge no Kunizuko]]''' *: A Kunizuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]]. He was demoted to the [[:en:Inagi|Inagi]] Rank due to his misconduct. His real name is unknown.
== Descendants ==
* [[Tsuge Mita]] *: Also known as Inabe Mita. A skilled craftsman during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], he was reputed for his acrobatics on tall buildings, appearing to "fly" as he moved.
* '''[[Kita Clan]]''' *: A prominent family in [[Yamabe District]], serving as the hereditary priestly family of [[Tsuge Mizukumi Shrine]]. Later, they adopted the surname Fujiwara.
